title: Otis and the Scared Little Bird
synopsis: When Otis finds a scared little bird hiding under a bush at his nature school, he learns how to be brave and kind by helping his new feathered friend find confidence and join the other birds in play.

pages.text: Otis loved his nature school. Every day, he and his friends played outside with the trees and flowers. Today, Otis heard a tiny peep-peep sound coming from under a green bush. He looked closer and saw a small yellow bird hiding in the shadows. The bird looked very scared.

illustration.description: A cheerful Black boy with short curly hair wearing a bright blue t-shirt and khaki shorts kneeling beside a leafy green bush. A small yellow bird with big round eyes peeks out nervously from under the bush's shadows. The scene is in a sunny outdoor nature school setting with colorful flowers and trees in the background.
illustration.alt: Otis discovering the scared little bird under a bush

pages.text: "Hello, little bird," Otis said softly. "Why are you hiding? Are you scared?" The bird's tiny head nodded up and down. "The other birds are too loud and fast for me," chirped the bird sadly. "I want to play, but I'm too scared to try." Otis felt sad for the little bird. He wanted to help.

illustration.description: Otis sitting cross-legged on grass, leaning forward with a gentle, caring expression on his face. The yellow bird sits on a small branch near the bush, looking down with droopy wings and sad eyes. Other colorful birds can be seen flying and playing in the background trees, appearing lively and energetic.
illustration.alt: Otis talking gently to the sad little bird

pages.text: "I have an idea!" said Otis happily. "Let's practice being brave together!" He stood up slowly and held out his hand. "First, we'll take one small step." The bird looked nervous but hopped onto Otis's palm. "See? You're already being brave!" Otis smiled. The bird felt a little less scared and a little more happy.

illustration.description: Otis standing with his hand extended, a big encouraging smile on his face. The small yellow bird sits carefully on his palm, looking slightly less frightened with one wing raised tentatively. Warm golden sunlight filters through green leaves above them, creating a hopeful and supportive atmosphere.
illustration.alt: The bird taking its first brave step onto Otis's hand

pages.text: Together, Otis and the bird practiced flying just a little bit each day. Soon, the bird felt brave enough to join the other birds! "Thank you, Otis," chirped the happy bird as it flew with its new friends. Otis felt proud and happy too. He had helped someone feel brave, and that made him feel very good inside.

illustration.description: A joyful scene showing the yellow bird flying confidently with several other colorful birds (red, blue, and green) around flowering tree branches. Otis stands below with his arms raised in celebration, beaming with pride. His diverse classmates including a Latina girl and an Asian boy cheer nearby. The background is filled with bright flowers and sunshine.
illustration.alt: The bird flying happily with other birds while Otis celebrates

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did the little bird feel at the beginning?
questions.explanation: The little bird was scared of the other birds because they were too loud and fast.

questions.question: What did Otis do to help the bird?
questions.explanation: Otis helped by practicing being brave with the bird, starting with small steps.

questions.question: How did Otis feel when he helped the bird?
questions.explanation: Otis felt happy and proud because he helped someone feel brave.

questions.question: What happened at the end of the story?
choices: The bird played with other birds
questions.explanation: At the end, the bird felt brave enough to fly and play with the other birds.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words!
pages.text: Otis found a _____ little bird hiding under a bush. The bird was too _____ to play with the other birds.

pages.title: Complete the Story!
pages.text: Otis helped the bird practice being _____. At the end, the bird felt _____ and flew with its friends.
